data.title: NIO Reaffirms Commitment to Europe as EU Moves Toward New Pricing Rules for Chinese EVs
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker NIO (NIO) said it is pushing ahead with its European expansion plans, even as the region reshapes how China‑made EVs are priced and regulated, Reuters reported. The company's comments came after the European Commission outlined new conditions that would allow Chinese EV manufacturers to replace existing tariffs with minimum price agreements.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, the Commission's new guidance signals a major shift in how Europe handles Chinese EV imports. Instead of relying on high tariffs, Europe will let manufacturers avoid those duties if they agree not to sell cars below a set minimum price. The key goal is to prevent unfair pricing while reducing friction between the two sides.

In a statement on Tuesday, NIO welcomed the progress in negotiations between Beijing and Brussels, saying, "We are pleased to see China and the EU making steady progress toward consensus on the basis of mutual respect."

NIO Expands Its European Footprint with New Markets

For NIO, the development offers a clearer path forward at a time when Chinese automakers are working to strengthen their foothold in Europe.

The company plans to enter seven additional European markets, Austria, Hungary, Belgium, Luxembourg, Poland, Romania, and the Czech Republic, by 2026. This marks a major step beyond its current footprint in Germany, the Netherlands, Denmark, Norway, and Sweden.

To scale quickly and efficiently, NIO is shifting from a purely direct-to-consumer model to a multi-channel strategy by partnering with major regional distributors. These partnerships give NIO access to established retail networks and service support, which is increasingly important as Europe's EV market becomes more competitive.

Is NIO a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on NIO stock, based on two Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. The average NIO stock price target of $5.90 implies 21.65% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: U.S. Futures Edge Lower Ahead of CPI Data and Major Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower Monday night as investors grew cautious ahead of key consumer inflation data and the first major bank earnings of the season. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.3%, 0.07%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 9:45 p.m. EDT on January 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In Monday's trading session, the S&P 500 closed with a 0.16% gain, while the Dow and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.17% and 0.08%.

Looking ahead, investors are focused on Tuesday's consumer price index (CPI) report, which is expected to give a clearer view of inflation after data gaps caused by last fall's long U.S. government shutdown.

At the same time, earnings season has started. Investors are watching for JPMorgan's (JPM) fourth-quarter earnings, due before the market opens on Tuesday. The report will kick off a wave of results from other major banks, including Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), and Morgan Stanley (MS), in the days ahead.

Together, these reports should give investors an early look at consumer spending, travel demand, trading activity, and deal flow as the year gets underway.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Cathie Wood Doubles Down on High-Potential Biotech Plays
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio moves on Monday, January 12, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The trades underscore ARK's ongoing focus on disruptive innovation in biotech, autonomous driving, blockchain, and fintech, while trimming slower-growth holdings.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood's Major Biotech Buys

ARK's largest purchase was 133,191 shares of GeneDx Holdings (WGS) for $15.88 million through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). GeneDx is a global leader in rare disease diagnosis with the largest such genomic dataset. WGS shares plunged 11.9% yesterday, after the company's guidance disappointed investors, despite robust preliminary FY25 results.

Similarly, Wood added 99,292 shares of clinical-stage biotech firm Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) for about $1.13 million, despite a 10.2% surge. She also added 49,963 shares of 10X Genomics (TXG), capitalizing on the 3.2% dip in its stock price. Finally, Wood bought 17,748 shares of Personalis (PSNL), consistent with her recent buying streak.

Wood Sells Mature Biotech Holdings

Wood offloaded 91,312 shares of Illumina (ILMN) for $13.29 million, signaling a shift toward earlier-stage genomics plays over established names. ILMN shares have surged nearly 48% over the past six months.

Additionally, Wood trimmed exposure to Natera (NTRA) by $2.61 million, Teradyne (TER) by $5.15 million, Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) by $1.11 million, and a minor sale in Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS).

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how some of these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on most of these stocks, with GeneDx stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Wells Fargo Is About to Report Its Q4 Earnings Tomorrow. Options Traders Are Expecting a 4.58% Move in WFC Stock
data.description:
Wells Fargo (WFC) is set to release its fourth quarter 2025 earnings results tomorrow, January 14. WFC stock has gained 35% in 2025, driven mainly by stronger earnings, steady cost cuts, and rising investor confidence in the bank's growth and capital return plans. According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 4.58% move in either direction for WFC stock in reaction to Q4 2025 results. This implied move is broadly in line with WFC stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 5% over the past four quarters.

data.title: Why Is Alibaba Stock (BABA) Up Over 10%?
data.description:
Shares of Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) climbed more than 10% on Monday, marking one of the stock's strongest single-day gains in months. The rally followed China's announcement of new steps to speed up the use of digital tools such as artificial intelligence across the economy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China's Industry and Information Technology Minister, Li Lecheng, said that the government will push faster adoption of AI in factories under a new "AI+ Manufacturing" plan. The remarks lifted Chinese tech stocks broadly, with investors viewing Alibaba as a key beneficiary of the policy push.

China Pushes AI and Tech Spending

The government also said it will roll out a new round of financial support in 2026 for smaller high-tech firms, often called "little giants," and encourage higher spending on research and development.

The goal is to lift tech self-reliance and drive new advances in areas such as AI, cloud systems, and next-gen manufacturing. Officials added that policy efforts will also focus on future tech fields, including quantum tech, robots, brain-computer links, and 6G.

BABA Stock Price Movement

Alibaba shares jumped in Monday's session but later slipped 0.33% in after-hours trading. Even so, the stock has delivered strong gains overall.

Shares are up about 106% over the past year and have climbed roughly 53% over the past six months, reflecting rising confidence in Alibaba's cloud business, AI push, and broader tech initiatives.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) to Lay Off 10% of Reality Labs Staff
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to cut 10% of the employees in its Reality Labs division, which houses work on the company's metaverse.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) was first to report the Reality Labs job cuts, which come as the company shifts its priorities to building artificial intelligence (AI) models and applications. The cuts to Reality Labs, which had 15,000 employees at the end of 2025, are a tiny percent of Meta's total workforce of 78,000 staff.

The job cuts, which will impact about 1,500 people, are likely to hit staff who work on Meta Platforms' virtual reality headsets and a virtual reality-based social network. The metaverse is a virtual reality realm where people interact with one another using Meta's Quest series of headsets.

Budget Cuts at Meta Platforms

Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg asked senior executives late last year to make cuts to their 2026 budgets, freeing up more money for AI research and engineering. Meta has increased the amount of money it is pouring into building AI superintelligence.

The company is projected to spend $109 billion on AI-related projects in 2026. Meta Platforms reportedly plans to hit the brakes on building virtual reality products for the metaverse. Meta's Reality Labs unit has lost nearly $75 billion to date as consumer interest in virtual reality remains tepid at best.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $824.71 implies 28.47% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Prove-It Year": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Gains as Tesla Faces Deadlines
data.description:
Give electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) credit for trying. It has imposed several major deadlines on itself this year, leading some to call 2026 Tesla's "Prove-It Year." That is a lot of pressure to put itself under, and improves the odds that it will disappoint somewhere along the line. Still, as the old saying says, if you aim for the moon and miss, the worst that happens is you end up among the stars. Investors took that notion to heart, and sent Tesla stock up nearly 2% in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This is a big year for Tesla. Advances in robotics, in self-driving technology, and in new vehicle design are all set to show up in 2026. The Roadster is expected to make a comeback, with an April 1 reveal date just in case something goes amiss. Cybercab volume production is set to kick in.

All of this sounds great, but there is one major concern as far as analysts are concerned: can Tesla's planned autonomous operations, backed up by artificial intelligence, actually scale? This led to Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein declaring, "I think 2026 is going to be the prove-it year for Tesla's robotaxi business. That's ultimately going to be the major driver for Tesla this year."

Another Door Lawsuit

Meanwhile, Tesla's gee-whiz door handles—particularly the aspects that make them difficult if not outright impossible to operate in an emergency—are drawing lawsuits again. A new class-action lawsuit alleges that the door handles on some Model S cars "routinely fail" after just a few years. One complaint noted that a 2015 Ludicrous variant owner saw three out of four door handles fail, requiring the driver to enter the vehicle from the passenger side, which featured the only remaining working door.

Apparently, a group of Tesla owners whose cars are 10 to 12 years old are discovering that the door handles are among the first things to go. This leaves owners unable to get in the vehicle they own, and may in turn prompt a major safety risk. Tesla, meanwhile, did not respond.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.34% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 10.2% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Gains as Deliveries Surge, More to Follow
data.description:
Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) is improving in a rapid fashion. The numbers are getting better, and in fact, Boeing recently called attention to a significant milestone about to hit: the highest number of airplane deliveries made in nearly a decade. This news was good enough for Boeing investors, who sent Boeing shares up nearly 2% in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing has not delivered this many airplanes in a year since 2018, reports note. That represents a significant achievement, especially given 2025 as a whole. Despite labor troubles in St. Louis and a Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) cap that stayed in place most of that year, Boeing still rolled out the aircraft and got them where they needed to be.

But Boeing is clearly making a comeback. AeroDynamic Advisory managing director Richard Aboulafia noted, "It's a long road back from a … shall we say, a rather dysfunctional culture, but they're making big progress." In fact, Boeing plans to step up production of the 737 MAX line and the 787 Dreamliner line as well. With reports that the 737 MAX 10 is getting closer to certification, this may represent another big step forward for Boeing.

New Examinations

There are also some signs that are less than positive for Boeing, reports note, as the FAA is looking to launch new examinations on some of the 737 lineup. Specifically, the FAA is looking at horizontal stabilizer components on several models of 737, including the 737-600, the 737-700, the 737-700C, the 737-800, the 737-900, and the 737-900ER.

The FAA has not issued an inspection requirement on the horizontal stabilizers yet. This includes both the pivot hinges and the jackscrew, reports note. But, "freeplay" in the horizontal stabilizer has been connected to reports of "pitch oscillation" events, which suggests a common link and a potential point of failure that will come up later.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 37.5% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $252.43 per share implies 5.7% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Vertiv Stock (VRT) Dip Opens Door for Thrifty Options Traders
data.description:
In the near term, Vertiv Holdings (VRT) appears to be a speculative bet. On paper, the company checks all the right boxes: it designs, manufactures, and services mission-critical digital infrastructure for data centers and communications networks. With artificial intelligence accelerating, VRT might seem like an obvious winner. Yet growing concerns over a potential AI bubble have cast a shadow across the sector, weighing on investor sentiment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That caution is already showing up in the price action. VRT shares are down roughly 4% to start the year and have fallen more than 8% over the past month. Even so, it would be a mistake to ignore the broader trend. Institutional capital continues to flow aggressively into machine learning and AI-driven infrastructure, and there's little evidence suggesting that momentum is poised to fade anytime soon.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Goldman Thinks Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Is Being Underestimated
data.description:
Top Goldman analyst Gabriela Borges initiated coverage on Microsoft (MSFT) with a Buy rating and a $655 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. Borges said investors' views on Microsoft's AI outlook have grown more cautious recently, but she believes those worries are overdone. In her view, Microsoft is better placed than most large tech firms as AI use grows across businesses and consumers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Goldman Is Bullish on Microsoft

The 4-star analyst pointed to Microsoft's reach across the AI stack, from cloud services to AI tools built into everyday products like Office and Windows. That wide presence gives the company more ways to grow as AI adoption picks up.

She also highlighted Microsoft's Copilot tools and AI agents, which are being rolled out across its products. She said this makes AI easier for customers to use and supports steady demand over time.

Because Microsoft controls both the platform and the software, Borges expects the company to deliver more stable growth than rivals that rely on a single product or partner. Looking ahead, Goldman expects that Microsoft could earn more than $35 per share by fiscal 2030 in a stronger scenario. That would mark faster earnings growth than most other large tech companies. 

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 15% rally in its share price over the past year, the average Microsoft price target of $631.36 per share implies 31.73% upside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
